name: Country Breakfast Sausage
directions: Combine all ingredients; mix well; cover and chill overnite.
Shape into 6 patties.
Cook in lightly greased skillet over medium heat about 15 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned on both sides and centers no longer pink.

name: Sage Breakfast Sausage
directions: Throughly combine all ingredients; shape into six patties.
Chill at least 1 hour.
Fry in a skillet for 3-4 minutes per side or until browned and no longer pink in the center.

name: Country Sausage
directions: Mix all ingredients really well and then grind in a sausage grinder.

name: Homemade Breakfast Sausage
directions: Combine the sage, salt, black pepper, marjoram, brown sugar, crushed red pepper and cloves.
Mix well.
Place the pork in a large bowl and add the mixed spices to it.
Mix well with your hands and form into 9 oblong rolls.
Wrap well in plastic wrap and chill.
(Freeze any that you aren't going to use within a week. Freezes up to three months) Slice off patties and cook as you normally would sausage.

name: Saged Up Sausage
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Mix well.
This is best after it's chilled a while.
It can also be frozen.
To fry, make patties and cook about 6 minutes per side on med-hi heat.

name: Breakfast Sausage
directions: Mix together all ingredients and form into patties.
Allow to rest 30 minutes in the refrigerator before use.
Heat pan over medium-high heat, and fry patties, flipping once.
About 7 minutes total.

name: Homemade Breakfast Sausage
directions: Mix the pork and seasoning together well and form into small patties.
You should be able to make 10 to 12, depending on size.
Fry about 5 minutes per side, and drain on paper towels.
Serve warm.

name: Country-Style Breakfast Sausage
directions: Mix meat and seasonings together.
Shape into 12 patties.
Spray skillet with non-stick spray.
Brown patties on each side.
Add a few tablespoons water to pan, cover to reserve moisture, and cook till done.
NOTE: Use this recipe in place of pork sausage for a healthier sausage stuffing for your turkey.

name: Homemade Breakfast Sausage
directions: In large bowl, mix ground meat and spices and herbs.
In warm water, add brown sugar or splenda and maple syrup if desired. Stir.
Add to ground meat mixture and mix well.
Form patties.
Fry in heavy skillet on medium heat. Brown on both sides.
Pack each pattie between waxed paper if you freeze. Then reheat in pan or microwave.

name: Breakfast Sausage
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Coat griddle or frypan with cooking spray (I like Crisco's olive oil spray).
Form into patties and brown each side for about 2 minutes on hot griddle.
Continue cooking over medium low until done.

name: Homemade Breakfast Sausage
directions: Put all the spices in a small bowl and mix them up with your fingers.
Work it into the pork after sprinkling it all over the meat. Try not to mash it around too much, otherwise the sausage will be too tight.

name: Spicy  Breakfast  Sausage
directions: Mix the pork, beef and veal thoroughly in a large mixing bowl.
Add the garlic, scallions, green pepper, cumin, thyme, fennel,.
nutmeg and red pepper flakes and salt to taste.
Mix thoroughly with your hands.
Fry a small amount in a skillet until no longer pink.
Taste for seasonings and adjust in the meat mixture if necessary.
Divide the meat mixture in half.  Roll each half into a log 7 inches long and about 2 inches in diameter.
Wrap the logs separately in plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 3 hours or up to 2 days.
Cut the sausage logs into 1/2 inch slices.
Fry the patties in a little bit of butter until well browned on both sides.
Serve with eggs, pancakes or French toast.

name: Breakfast Turkey Sausage Patties
directions: Combine the turkey and dry spices in a large bowl.  Mix together thoroughly.
Add the chicken broth, mixing well.  Let stand for 15 to 20 minutes.
Form the turkey into 8 patties, approximately 3/4 inch thick.
Cook the patties in a nonstick skillet over medium heat for 7 to 8 minutes on each side or grill.  However, if you grill these, be sure to pray the grill grate with oil prior to grilling.

name: Turkey Breakfast Sausage
directions: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients, except oil, and mix well.
Heat a little oil in a skillet. (We use olive oil).
Test a little by forming a little patty and frying it up until cooked through.
At this point you can adjust your seasonings by adding salt or pepper, hot pepper flakes, maple syrup -- accordingly.

name: Homemade Breakfast Sausage
directions: Spread the pork out in a large bowl and sprinkle with the maple syrup, garlic (if using), salt, pepper, sage, thyme, and cayenne. Using your hands, gently fold the flavorings evenly into the pork, then portion and shape into sixteen 2-ounce patties (about 1/4 cup each).

Heat 2 teaspoons of the oil in a 12-inch nonstick skillet over medium heat until shimmering. Add half of the patties and cook until well browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side. Transfer to a paper-towel-lined plate. Wipe out the skillet, add the remaining 2 teaspoons oil, return to medium heat until shimmering, and cook the remaining patties.

name: Turkey Breakfast Sausage
directions: Crumble turkey into a large bowl.   Add the salt, sage, pepper and ginger.  Shape into eight 2 inch patties.   In a nonstick skillet coated with nonstick cooking spray, cook patties over medium heat for 6-8 minutes on each side or until   no longer pink and cooked well.

name: Breakfast Sausage
directions: In a mixing bowl add all ingredients EXCEPT pork and mix well.
Place pork in a large mixing bowl and add spice mixture.
Blend by hand for about 3-5 minutes or until thoroughly blended.
Stuff it in casings or simply make patties and fry it or grill it.
If you start with lean ground pork you will have lean sausage.
If your market does not have ground pork, ask your butcher to grind some for you, or do what I did, buy your own grinder.
Freezes well so increase the recipe.

name: Juicy Turkey Breakfast Sausage
directions: Crumble ground turkey into a large mixing bowl.

Tear bread into large pieces and place in food processor.  Add onions and all seasonings.  Pulse until everything is in uniform crumbs.  Add chicken broth and pulse until combined.  The amount of broth will depend on the dryness of your bread - it should have the consistency of oatmeal.  (And yes, it looks pretty disgusting).
Add bread mixture to the turkey in the bowl and combine thoroughly (I use my hands for this).  Cover and refrigerate overnight to let flavors blend.
Heat olive oil in skillet.  Shape sausage into 16 patties and fry until cooked through.

name: Apple Pork Breakfast Sausage
directions: Grate apple using a cheese grater.
Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix using hands to thoroughly integrate all the ingredients.
Form into links or patties.

name: Country-Style Beef Breakfast Sausage
directions: For fullest flavor, let these sausages sit overnight after mixed.
Mix all spices into the meat and stir well with a wooden spoon, or hands.
Form patties and cook like a hamburger in a skillet over medium high heat.
To make sure the sausage is juicy "sear" both sides (on high heat) then reduce the the heat (medium or medium high) and finish the cooking. The well cooked outside will hold in the juices.

name: Homemade Breakfast Sausage
directions: Note: Mix the dry ingredients first before you add them to the meat.
In a small bowl, combine the sage, salt, ground black pepper, marjoram, brown sugar, crushed red pepper and breadcrumbs. Mix well.
Place the pork in a large bowl and add the mixed spices to it. Mix well with your hands and form into patties. No thicker than 1/4 inch.
Cook the patties in a large skillet over medium heat for 5 minutes per side, or until internal pork temperature reaches 160 degrees F.
Note:  You can make the patties ahead of time and freeze for later.
Note:  You can substitute the ground pork with ground turkey breast.

name: Breakfast Sausage-Market Style
directions: Mix ground meat, spices and herbs in large bowl.
In warm water, add brown sugar and maple syrup, stir to mix well.
Add to ground meat and mix well.
Place in refrigerator for 30 minutes to chill.
Form patties.  Can use a round egg mold and saucer to make uniform size.
Fry in heavy skillet on medium heat.  Brown both sides.
Pack each patties in plastic wrap and freeze.
Reheat in pan and serve with egg scramble or dice and add to scramble.
OPTIONS and/or SUBSTITIONS:
1 samll onion, fine diced.
2 garlic cloves, minced.
2 tablespoons cilantro.
2 tablespoons fresh chives.
1/2 tsp dried basil.
1/2 tsp dried oregano.
1/2 tsp ground cayenne.

name: Homemade Turkey Breakfast Sausage
directions: Combine ground meat with all ingredients, up to and including the nutmeg.  Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 1 hour or overnight.  Form the mixture into 8 patties or links if you prepfer.  Two ounc each.  When ready to cook.  Spray a skillet with cooking spray and heat over med-high heat.  Add the oil.  When the pot is hot, add the patties and cook for about 4 minutes.  Spray the top of the patties and flip, to cook the other side.  Cook for an additional 4 minutes or until done.  Transfer the patties to a plate lined with paper towel to drain.  Serve and Enjoy.  1 serving equals to patties.

name: Brown Sugar Breakfast Sausage
directions: Cut your pork into about 1 inch cubes and place in a large bowl.
In a small bowl combine all of your spices, herbs, brown sugar, salt & pepper and mix well.
Pour spice mixture over your cubed meat and mix well with your hands.
Cover bowl and refrigerate for 4 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to meld.
Using a meat grinder grind your seasoned pork cubes at your desired coarseness.
Stuff into sausage casings.
